channel.title: CyberNetSec - Security Operations
channel.description: Latest security operations threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: CISA Adds Four Actively Exploited Flaws in DD-WRT, Langflow, WordPress
item.description:
On July 21, 2026, the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added four new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, indicating they are under active attack. The flaws include a buffer overflow in DD-WRT (CVE-2021-27137), an untrusted control sphere...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: Cisco Releases Open-Weight 'Antares' AI Models for Code Analysis
item.description:
Cisco has released Antares, a family of open-weight small language models (SLMs) specifically trained to perform static application security testing (SAST) and identify security vulnerabilities in source code. The models, including Antares-1.3B and Antares-7B, are designed to run locally on standard...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: ReliaQuest and OpenAI Partner to Advance AI in Cyber Defense
item.description:
Cybersecurity firm ReliaQuest announced on July 19, 2026, that it is joining the OpenAI Daybreak Cyber Partner Program. This partnership aims to accelerate the development of advanced AI capabilities for enterprise cyber defense, providing ReliaQuest with access to OpenAI's frontier models to integr...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Report: Enterprises Overwhelmed by Disjointed Security Tools Amid AI Risks
item.description:
A report from Thales reveals that enterprises are using a multitude of separate security tools, leading to policy inconsistencies and visibility gaps. This 'tool sprawl,' where organizations use an average of seven different tools for data protection alone, is creating a significant and expanding at...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Cloud Security, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →

item.title: New security tools target deepfakes, risky OAuth, and AI agent threats
item.description:
This week saw the launch of several new security products aimed at addressing emerging enterprise threats. Polygraf AI introduced 'Meeting Guard' for real-time deepfake detection in video meetings. Nudge Security released capabilities to automate the discovery and remediation of risky OAuth grants a...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Cloud Security, Other

Read Full Article →

item.title: SeasonalInvite Phishing Campaign Abuses Legitimate RMM Tools
item.description:
A phishing campaign named 'SeasonalInvite' has been using fake seasonal eCard invitations to trick users into installing legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) tools. Active since January 2026, the campaign abuses trusted software like ConnectWise, LogMeIn, and Kaseya to gain persistent r...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Phishing, Cyberattack, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: WatchGuard: Shadow AI, Password Reuse Fueling Business Cyber Risk
item.description:
A July 2026 report from WatchGuard Technologies finds that employee behaviors are creating significant, unmanaged cybersecurity risks for businesses. The study reveals a pervasive "shadow AI" problem, with 64% of employees using unauthorized AI tools for work. This is compounded by persistent poor s...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Phishing, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →

item.title: AI Increases Need for Human Oversight in Cyber Roles, ISC2 Finds
item.description:
A new report from ISC2 finds that artificial intelligence is transforming cybersecurity roles by increasing the need for human validation and governance, rather than simply replacing jobs. The study, released July 14, 2026, shows that 63% of cybersecurity professionals now spend more time reviewing ...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Policy and Compliance, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Canada Deploys National Cybersecurity Sensors to Protect Northern Governments
item.description:
Canada's Communications Security Establishment (CSE) is now operating cybersecurity sensors across the IT systems of all three of its northern territories—Yukon, Northwest Territories, and Nunavut. The deployment, completed in 2024, aims to block malicious activity from cybercriminals and foreign go...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Policy and Compliance, Incident Response

Read Full Article →

item.title: Attackers Use Fake Microsoft Teams IT Support Calls to Push EtherRAT Malware
item.description:
A live social engineering campaign is targeting corporate users by impersonating IT support staff over Microsoft Teams voice calls. According to Unit 42, attackers initiate contact with a phishing email and follow up with a voice call from an external Teams tenant. They socially engineer the victim ...

Severity: High

Categories: Phishing, Malware, Security Operations

Read Full Article →
